sourceMovie.title: The Lincoln Lawyer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Lincoln Lawyer (2011) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Brad Furman's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Lincoln Lawyer clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Lincoln Lawyer?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, The Talented Mr. Ripley, Primal Fear—each shares DNA with The Lincoln Lawyer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Lincoln Lawyer?
faq.answer: The Lincoln Lawyer (2011) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Lincoln Lawyer worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
